summary: CLED medium supports the growth of Gram-positive bacteria like Staphylococcus and fungi such as Candida, whereas MacConkey agar inhibits their growth due to bile salts and crystal violet. This makes CLED more suitable for isolating pathogens from patients with infections involving these types of microorganisms.
